provenance.source_corpus: Distribution and potential effects of water beetles in lakes recovering from acidification

source_spans.text: In many of the lakes, ﬁsh populations were lost because of acid deposition originating from nearby metal smelters
In many of the lakes, ﬁsh populations were lost because of acid deposition originating from nearby metal smelters
In the absence of ﬁsh, beetle biomass is high, individual sizes are large and, as found in our study, pelagic larvae such as those of the dytiscid species G. liberus are abundant
In the absence of ﬁsh, beetle biomass is high, individual sizes are large and, as found in our study, pelagic larvae such as those of the dytiscid species G. liberus are abundant
Mesocosm experiments in Swan Lake, a ﬁshless lake recovering from acidiﬁcation, revealed that larval G. liberus can reduce total zooplankton abundance, species richness, and species diversity.
Mesocosm experiments in Swan Lake, a ﬁshless lake recovering from acidiﬁcation, revealed that larval G. liberus can reduce total zooplankton abundance, species richness, and species diversity.
Mesocosm experiments in Swan Lake, a ﬁshless lake recovering from acidiﬁcation, revealed that larval G. liberus can reduce total zooplankton abundance, species richness, and species diversity.
Mesocosm experiments in Swan Lake, a ﬁshless lake recovering from acidiﬁcation, revealed that larval G. liberus can reduce total zooplankton abundance, species richness, and species diversity.
When we used forward stepwise selection of the 7 environmental variables in the RDA, only ﬁsh presence/absence explained a signiﬁcant amount of variation in water beetle composition (8%, p = 0.002).
When we used forward stepwise selection of the 7 environmental variables in the RDA, only ﬁsh presence/absence explained a signiﬁcant amount of variation in water beetle composition (8%, p = 0.002).

The high abundance of G. liberus in the absence of ﬁsh and the impact of larval G. liberus predation on crustacean zooplankton in Swan Lake suggest that biological resistance may be an important impediment to the recovery of aquatic food webs, despite increasing pH.
Water beetle richness was signiﬁcantly related to pH (R2 = 0.18, p = 0.02; Fig. 2).
Water beetle richness was signiﬁcantly related to pH (R2 = 0.18, p = 0.02; Fig. 2).
